channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Six Months On, Search Continues for Suspect in Fatal Shooting of Two Australian Police Officers
item.description: Six months after two police officers were fatally shot in Porepunkah, a small tourist town in Victoria, Australia, the search for the main suspect, Dezi Freeman, remains unresolved. Freeman, whose rea...

item.title: Titan and Saturn's Rings: A Collision Between Ancient Moons
item.description: New research suggests that Saturn's largest moon, Titan, may have formed from a massive collision between two earlier moons. This cosmic crash could explain Titan's unusual orbit and its surprisingly ...

item.title: Potential US-Russia Deal May Involve Nord Stream Pipeline and Ukraine Settlement
item.description: The Trump administration is reportedly exploring a settlement to the ongoing Ukraine war, with economic incentives for Russia and potential pressure on Ukraine’s leadership. One major aspect under dis...

item.title: Spain alerts WHO after suspected case of human-to-human swine flu transmission in Catalonia
item.description: Spain has notified the World Health Organisation (WHO) of a suspected case of human-to-human transmission of the A(H1N1)v swine flu variant in the region of Catalonia. According to regional health aut...

item.title: Investigation of Xiaomi Car Accident Reveals Doors Failed After Power Loss
item.description: A Xiaomi vehicle accident in Chengdu, China, has raised serious safety concerns after investigation reports revealed that the car’s doors could not be opened due to a low-voltage system failure. The c...

item.title: Driver jailed after gambling on phone causes crash that led to premature birth
item.description: A 30-year-old man has been jailed after causing a serious road collision while gambling on his mobile phone, resulting in a pregnant woman giving birth prematurely. Jack Bentley crashed into a station...

item.title: Family of Ruben Ray Martinez Seeks Transparency After Fatal ICE Shooting in Texas
item.description: The mother of 23-year-old Ruben Ray Martinez, who was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent in South Padre Island, Texas, voted for Donald Trump in the 2024 presidential e...

item.title: Government Review Says Sizewell C Electricity Could Cost £120/MWh, Sparking Debate Over Consumer Impact
item.description: Electricity generated by the planned Sizewell C nuclear power station in Suffolk is expected to cost around £120 per megawatt hour (MWh), according to a Government review, nearly double current wholes...

item.title: Pro-Palestine Protesters Demonstrate at ‘Scream 7’ Premiere Over Melissa Barrera’s Removal
item.description: Pro-Palestine activists gathered outside Paramount Pictures Studios in Los Angeles during the premiere of Scream 7 to protest the firing of actress Melissa Barrera and to call for a boycott of the fil...

item.title: Kansas to Invalidate Driver’s Licenses with Updated Gender Markers Under New State Law
item.description: Transgender residents in Kansas are being notified that their driver’s licenses will become invalid as a new state law takes effect Thursday. The Kansas Department of Revenue (KDOR) has mailed letters...

item.title: Anthropic Affirms Defense Collaboration but Refuses Mass Surveillance and Fully Autonomous Weapons Uses
item.description: In a public statement, Dario Amodei of Anthropic outlines the company’s position regarding its collaboration with the U.S. Department of War and the broader national security community. He emphasizes ...

item.title: Ant and Dec say challenging audience behaviour put them off hosting the Brit Awards again
item.description: Ant McPartlin and Declan Donnelly have said they will not host the Brit Awards again, citing difficulties they experienced during previous ceremonies. The long-standing presenting duo, who fronted the...

item.title: Brazil’s Supreme Court Sentences Brazão Brothers to Over 76 Years for Ordering Marielle Franco’s Murder
item.description: Brazil's Supreme Court has convicted brothers Domingos Inácio Brazão and João Francisco Inácio Brazão for ordering the 2018 murder of Rio de Janeiro city councillor and activist Marielle Franco. Each ...

item.title: Green Party overturns long-held Labour seat in Gorton and Denton by-election
item.description: The Green Party has secured a historic victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election, overturning a long-standing Labour majority and pushing the governing party into third place. Hannah Spencer, a loc...

item.title: Documentary Explores the Bedrooms of Children Lost to US School Shootings
item.description: All the Empty Rooms is an Oscar-nominated short documentary by CBS correspondent Steve Hartman that explores the bedrooms of children killed in US school shootings. The 34-minute film, photographed by...

item.title: Mexican Cartel Leader ‘El Mencho’ Killed After Military Operation in Tapalpa
item.description: Tapalpa, a picturesque tourist town in Jalisco state known for its pine forests and cobbled streets, has been left shaken after a dramatic military operation resulted in the death of notorious cartel ...

item.title: Charity kitchen volunteers targeted and killed as Sudan’s conflict deepens humanitarian crisis
item.description: Nearly three years into Sudan’s devastating conflict between the Sudanese army and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), community-run charity kitchens have become a vital lifeline for civilians facing fami...

item.title: Champions League Round of 16 Draw Sets Potential English Matchups and Key Fixtures
item.description: With the Champions League play-offs concluded, the remaining teams are set to learn their routes through the round of 16. Paris Saint-Germain narrowly defeated Monaco 5-4 on aggregate, while Real Madr...

item.title: Daniel Hannan criticises Green Party tactics in Gorton and Denton by-election
item.description: In this opinion piece, Daniel Hannan argues that the Green Party’s victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election marks a troubling moment in British politics. He contends that the party’s campaign stra...

item.title: Scientists Develop High-Dimensional Quantum Light for Advanced Communication
item.description: Physicists at the University of the Witwatersrand in South Africa, in collaboration with the Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona, have demonstrated innovative methods to manipulate light at the quantum ...
